children.heading: Limitation on support for single entity
children.label: a Limitation on support for single entity
children.text: No entity receiving support from the Corporation under subchapter II of this chapter may receive more than an amount equal to 2.5 percent of the Corporation’s maximum contingent liability authorized under .section 9633 of this title

children.heading: Preference for support for projects sponsored by United States persons
children.label: b Preference for support for projects sponsored by United States persons

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Corporation should give preferential consideration to projects sponsored by or involving private sector entities that are United States persons.

children.label: A
children.text: a United States citizen; or

children.label: B
children.text: an entity owned or controlled by an individual or individuals described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Preference for support in countries in compliance with international trade obligations
children.label: c Preference for support in countries in compliance with international trade obligations

children.heading: Consultations with United States Trade Representative
children.label: 1 Consultations with United States Trade Representative
children.text: Not less frequently than annually, the Corporation shall consult with the United States Trade Representative with respect to the status of countries eligible to receive support from the Corporation under subchapter II of this chapter and the compliance of those countries with their international trade obligations.

children.heading: Preferential consideration
children.label: 2 Preferential consideration
children.text: The Corporation shall give preferential consideration to providing support under subchapter II of this chapter for projects in countries in compliance with or making substantial progress coming into compliance with their international trade obligations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Corporation shall only support projects under subchapter II of this chapter in countries that are taking steps to adopt and implement laws that extend internationally recognized worker rights (as defined in ) to workers in that country, including any designated zone in that country.section 2467 of title 19

children.heading: Required contract language
children.label: 2 Required contract language
children.text: The Corporation shall also include the following language, in substantially the following form, in all contracts which the Corporation enters into with persons receiving support under subchapter II of this chapter: “The person receiving support agrees not to take actions to prevent employees of the foreign enterprise from lawfully exercising their right of association and their right to organize and bargain collectively. The person further agrees to observe applicable laws relating to a minimum age for employment of children, acceptable conditions of work with respect to minimum wages, hours of work, and occupational health and safety, and not to use forced labor or the worst forms of child labor (as defined in section 507 of the Trade Act of 1974 ()). The person is not responsible under this paragraph for the actions of a foreign government.”.19 U.S.C. 2467

at least 60 days before the date of the vote, an environmental and social impact assessment or initial environmental and social audit, analyzing the environmental and social impacts of the proposed project and of alternatives to the proposed project, including mitigation measures, is completed;

such assessment or audit has been made available to the public of the United States, locally affected groups in the country in which the project will be carried out, and nongovernmental organizations in that country; and

the Corporation, applying best practices with respect to environmental and social safeguards, includes in any contract relating to the project provisions to ensure the mitigation of any such adverse environmental or social impacts.

children.heading: Women’s economic empowerment
children.label: f Women’s economic empowerment
children.text: In utilizing its authorities under subchapter II of this chapter, the Corporation shall consider the impacts of its support on women’s economic opportunities and outcomes and shall prioritize the reduction of gender gaps and maximize development impact by working to improve women’s economic opportunities.

children.heading: Preference for provision of support in countries embracing private enterprise
children.label: g Preference for provision of support in countries embracing private enterprise
A
children.text: market-based economic policies;

children.label: B
children.text: protection of private property rights;

children.label: C
children.text: respect for the rule of law; and

children.label: D
children.text: systems to combat corruption and bribery.

children.heading: Sources of information
children.label: 2 Sources of information
children.text: The Corporation should rely on both third-party indicators and United States Government information, such as the Department of State’s Investment Climate Statements, the Department of Commerce’s Country Commercial Guides, or the Millennium Challenge Corporation’s Constraints Analysis, to assess whether countries meet the conditions described in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Consideration of foreign boycott participation
children.label: h Consideration of foreign boycott participation
children.text: In providing support for projects under subchapter II of this chapter, the Corporation shall consider, using information readily available, whether the project is sponsored by or substantially affiliated with any person taking or knowingly agreeing to take actions, or having taken or knowingly agreed to take actions within the past 3 years, which demonstrate or otherwise evidence intent to comply with, further, or support any boycott described in .section 4842(a) of title 50

children.heading: Ensuring opportunities for small businesses in foreign development
children.label: i Ensuring opportunities for small businesses in foreign development

children.text: to give preferential consideration in providing support under subchapter II of this chapter to projects sponsored by or involving small businesses; and

to ensure that the proportion of projects sponsored by or involving United States small businesses, including women-, minority-, and veteran-owned small businesses, is not less than 50 percent of all projects for which the Corporation provides support and that involve United States persons.

children.heading: Policies with respect to state-owned enterprises, anticompetitive practices, and countries of concern
children.label: j Policies with respect to state-owned enterprises, anticompetitive practices, and countries of concern

children.heading: Policy
children.label: 1 Policy
children.text: The Corporation shall develop appropriate policies and guidelines for support provided under subchapter II of this chapter for a project involving a state-owned enterprise, sovereign wealth fund, or a parastatal entity to ensure such support is provided consistent with appropriate principles and practices of competitive neutrality.

children.heading: Anticompetitive practices
children.label: A Anticompetitive practices
children.text: The Corporation may not provide support under subchapter II of this chapter for a project that involves a private sector entity engaged in anticompetitive practices.

children.heading: Countries of concern
children.label: B Countries of concern
children.text: The Corporation may not provide support under subchapter II of this chapter for projects that would be operated, managed, or controlled by the government of a county of concern or a state-owned enterprise that belongs to or is under the control of a country of concern.

children.label: i
children.text: a certification, which may be included as a classified or confidential annex to a report required by , that such support is important to the national security interests of the United States; andsection 9656 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a written justification of how such support directly counters or significantly limits the influence of an entity described in such subparagraph.

children.label: i
children.text: the level of ownership; and

children.label: ii
children.text: whether or not the power is exercised.

children.heading: Owned
children.label: B Owned
children.text: The term “owned”, with respect to an enterprise, means a majority or controlling interest, whether by value or voting interest, of the shares of that enterprise, including through fiduciaries, agents, or other means.

children.heading: State-owned enterprise
children.label: C State-owned enterprise
children.text: The term “state-owned enterprise” means any enterprise established for a commercial or business purpose that is directly owned or controlled by one or more governments, including any agency, instrumentality, subdivision, or other unit of government at any level of jurisdiction.
